title: Horticultural trades
description: Work with agricultural scientists in plant, fiber, and animal research, or assist with animal breeding and nutrition. Set up or maintain laboratory equipment and collect samples from crops or animals. Prepare specimens or record data to assist scientists in biology or related life science experiments. Conduct tests and experiments to improve yield and quality of crops or to increase the resistance of plants and animals to disease or insects. Apply geospatial technologies, including geographic information systems (GIS) and Global Positioning System (GPS), to agricultural production or management activities, such as pest scouting, site-specific pesticide application, yield mapping, or variable-rate irrigation. May use computers to develop or analyze maps or remote sensing images to compare physical topography with data on soils, fertilizer, pests, or weather. Work with food scientists or technologists to perform standardized qualitative and quantitative tests to determine physical or chemical properties of food or beverage products. Includes technicians who assist in research and development of production technology, quality control, packaging, processing, and use of foods. (Based on 12 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Record data pertaining to experimentation, research, or animal care, and document and maintain records of precision agriculture information.
Analyze remote sensing imagery and geospatial data to identify relationships between soil quality, crop yields, and weather conditions, determining agricultural implications.
Prepare and incubate laboratory samples for analysis and perform laboratory or field testing using various equipment to evaluate crops and other biological samples.
Set up laboratory or field equipment as required for site testing and maintain records of testing results or other documents as required by governing agencies.
Collect samples from crops or animals and examine specimens to determine the presence of diseases or other problems.
Train newly hired laboratory personnel and supervise agricultural technicians or farm laborers in their duties.
Prepare land for cultivation, including tasks such as tilling, hoeing, and watering, and operate farm machinery.
Use geospatial technology to develop soil sampling grids, identify sampling sites, and program farm equipment based on crop scouting analysis.
Create and analyze maps showing precision agricultural data, including crop characteristics, yield patterns, and other factors.
Advise farmers on pest management, pesticide treatments, and upgrading GPS equipment for improved agricultural practices.
Perform tests on food and beverage products, including quality control assessments and compliance with standards and regulations.
Harvest plants or crops, ensuring proper techniques for transplanting, potting, and labeling.
Collect debris and refuse from agricultural operations, and clean work areas alongside maintaining grounds and landscaping.
Operate vehicles or powered equipment for landscaping and agricultural tasks, including mowing, trimming, and irrigation setup.
Supervise pest or weed control operations, and respond to public inquiries regarding agricultural practices.
Provide information about crop progress to farmers or farm managers and participate in the inspection and grading of crops.
Analyze data from harvester monitors to develop yield maps and integrate this data into agricultural practices for improved productivity.
Maintain tallies of trees examined and counted during tree marking or measuring efforts.
Spray or inject vegetation with insecticides to kill insects or to protect against disease, or use herbicides to reduce competing vegetation.
Identify and remove diseased or undesirable trees using power saws or hand saws.
Select or cut trees according to markings, sizes, types, or grades.

Perform fire protection or suppression duties, such as constructing fire breaks or disposing of brush, and fight forest fires or perform prescribed burning tasks under the direction of fire suppression officers or forestry technicians.
Confer with other workers to discuss safety issues, cutting heights, or work needs.
Check equipment to ensure proper operation, and adjust, repair, and service machinery as needed.
Direct activities of workers who perform landscaping, cultivate lawns, or prune trees and shrubs; train workers in tasks such as transplanting, pruning, feeding animals, and operating equipment.
Direct or perform the mixing and application of fertilizers, insecticides, herbicides, or fungicides, including spraying fertilizer or pesticide solutions to control insects, fungus, and weed growth.
Perform personnel-related activities such as hiring workers, evaluating performances, or taking disciplinary actions when performance problems occur.
Establish and enforce operating procedures and work standards that ensure adequate performance and personnel safety.
Inspect completed work to ensure it meets specifications, standards, and contract requirements.
Recommend changes to improve working conditions or equipment to increase crew efficiency, and negotiate with customers regarding fees for landscaping or groundskeeping work.
Schedule work for crews based on priorities, availability, and weather conditions; monitor project activities to ensure adherence to instructions and deadlines.
Maintain required records such as personnel information, project records, or activity reports.
Review contracts or work assignments to determine service, machine, or workforce requirements for jobs; inventory supplies of tools and materials to ensure availability and usability.
Prepare service estimates based on labor, material, and machinery costs.
Tour and inspect grounds such as parks or botanical gardens for plant and soil conditions; inspect facilities to determine maintenance needs.
Design or supervise installation of irrigation and sprinkler systems, and irrigate soil using various methods.
Observe and monitor health of crops, animals, and aquatic life; treat illnesses or injuries as needed, and investigate work-related complaints to verify and determine responses.
Coordinate or assist in dismantling, moving, and setting up equipment at work sites and manage transport of animals, supplies, and workers.
Monitor operations to identify problems and ensure compliance with safety, company, and government regulations.
Plan and schedule work according to personnel and equipment availability; confer with managers to evaluate conditions, develop plans, and discuss techniques.
Inspect crops, fields, or plant stock to determine conditions and needs for cultivation, spraying, weeding, or harvesting.
Operate or tend machinery used in agricultural production such as tractors and irrigation equipment; attach implements to equipment as necessary.
Record various agricultural data, such as weights and types of fish or crop-filled containers, managing daily activities.
Perform miscellaneous tasks such as directing or assisting workers in maintenance, ordering performance corrections, and facilitating good communication between teams.
Issue and manage the usage of tools and equipment, and proactively ensure they are in working condition for projects.
Conduct administrative duties such as processing time sheets and managing budgets for maintenance or development of operations.
Observe and follow machinery operation to detect and resolve malfunctions as necessary.
Monitor workers to ensure compliance with safety regulations and provide guidance as necessary.

emerging_tasks.task: Operate drone technology to capture aerial imagery and data for crop monitoring and analysis.
Create field maps using geographic information systems technology.
Operate drones to monitor crop health, growth and pest infestations, and apply targeted treatments.
